meta.docs:
# Pagination

A presentational `<nav>` of page links ‚Äî anchor-based, server-friendly, and unstyled by data semantics. Use to navigate between pre-computed pages where you own the page-number list and active-page state.

## When to use

- **Page navigation** ‚Äî compose `Pagination` + `PaginationContent` + `<PaginationItem>` per page with `<PaginationLink href isActive>`.
- **Previous / Next chevrons** ‚Äî render `<PaginationPrevious href>` and `<PaginationNext href>` at the ends.
- **Skipped page ranges** ‚Äî drop `<PaginationEllipsis />` between non-contiguous numbers.
- **Active page marker** ‚Äî `isActive` on a `<PaginationLink>` sets `aria-current="page"` and switches to the `outline` button variant.
- **Router integration** ‚Äî `<PaginationLink>` is a real `<a>`; pass router-aware props or wrap with your router's `<Link>` component.

## When not to use

- **Carbon-style "1‚Äì10 of 103 / items per page" footer** ‚Äî no shadcn primitive ships these. Compose a sibling `<Select>` for page-size and a `<span>` for the range yourself.
- **Cursor-based / infinite-scroll lists** ‚Äî render a "Load more" `Button` instead.
- **Two pages or fewer** ‚Äî render plain prev/next `Button`s without a numbered list.

## Composition

- This is a **presentational** primitive ‚Äî there is no `totalItems`, `pageSize`, or `onChange`. Compute the page-number list (which numbers to show, where to insert ellipses) in your own component.
- Disable the boundaries: at page 1 omit `<PaginationPrevious>` (or set `aria-disabled="true" tabIndex={-1}`); at last page do the same for `<PaginationNext>`.
- Default layout is centered (`mx-auto flex w-full justify-center`); restyle with `className` for left/right alignment.
meta.migrationGuide:
# Pagination ‚Äî Carbon ‚Üí shadcn migration guide ## They solve different problems This is the biggest gap on the list. Carbon's `Pagination` and shadcn's `Pagination` overlap in name only. - **Carbon `Pagination`** is a **stateful data-table footer**. Give it `totalItems`, `pageSize`, `pageSizes` and it renders: page-size selector, "1‚Äì10 of 103 items" text, page input/dropdown, and prev/next buttons. It manages all the pagination math internally. - **shadcn `Pagination`** is a **presentational `
` of links**. You pre-compute which page numbers to show and feed in `` / `` / `` / `` yourself. There is no `totalItems`, no `pageSize`, no page-size selector. If your app uses Carbon's `Pagination` to drive a table, swapping to shadcn's `Pagination` requires you to **own the state and derived UI** (which page is active, how many to show, when to show ellipses, and whether to render a page-size selector at all ‚Äî there is no shadcn primitive for that last part). ## Structural differences | Concept | Carbon | shadcn | |---|---|---| | Wrapper | `` (single component) | `‚Ä¶` (composed) | | Page list | implicit (prev / page-input / next) | explicit `` per page | | Active page | `page` prop | `` per item | | Prev / Next | implicit; `backwardText` / `forwardText` props | `` / `` (anchor-based) | | Ellipsis ("‚Ä¶") | not modeled (Carbon uses a numeric input or dropdown to jump pages) | `` | | Page-size selector | built-in, controlled by `pageSizes` + `pageSize` + `onChange({pageSize})` | none ‚Äî render your own `` next to the pagination. - **"X‚ÄìY of Z items" range text** ‚Äî Carbon localizes and renders this. shadcn does not ‚Äî render `{(page - 1) * pageSize + 1}‚Äì{Math.min(page * pageSize, total)} of {total}` yourself. - **Page-input vs. page-dropdown** ‚Äî Carbon switches between an input field (for many pages) and a dropdown (for few). shadcn has no equivalent ‚Äî you decide what to show in ``. - **`pagesUnknown` / `pageInputDisabled` / `pageSizeInputDisabled`** ‚Äî feature flags for partial UIs. No shadcn equivalent. - **Skeleton state (`PaginationSkeleton`)** ‚Äî Carbon ships a skeleton variant. shadcn has none ‚Äî use the generic `skeleton` primitive. - **`isLastPage` boolean** ‚Äî Carbon disables `Next` once on the last page. shadcn requires you to omit / `aria-disabled` `` yourself. - **`backwardText` / `forwardText` / `itemsPerPageText` / `itemRangeText` (i18n hooks)** ‚Äî Carbon centralises label strings. shadcn hardcodes "Previous" / "Next" / "More pages" in English; rewrite or pass children. - **Built-in keyboard handling** ‚Äî Carbon's input/dropdown handle arrow keys and Enter. shadcn delegates to plain `` semantics. ## shadcn features missing in Carbon - **Anchor-based, server-friendly** ‚Äî `` works without JS. SEO-friendly out of the box. Carbon's pagination is JS-only. - **`isActive` prop** ‚Äî explicit current-page marker that maps to `aria-current="page"`. Cleaner semantics than Carbon's "the current page is whatever `page` is". - **Composability** ‚Äî drop `` wherever you want, render only the numbers you care about, intermix custom buttons. Carbon's prev/input/next layout is fixed. - **`asChild`-style swap** ‚Äî pass router-library `` via children to integrate with Next.js / React Router; Carbon needs a wrapper component. - **No CSS-in-JS dependency** ‚Äî pure Tailwind classes via `buttonVariants`; tweak with `className`. ## Behavioural differences - **Default element** ‚Äî Carbon: `
` shell with form controls inside. shadcn: `
` wrapping a `
    ` of `
  • ` anchors. - **Layout** ‚Äî Carbon: full-width footer that hugs a table. shadcn: centered horizontally (`mx-auto flex w-full justify-center`); restyle if you need left/right alignment. - **Variant of "current" link** ‚Äî shadcn renders the active page as `outline` button variant; non-active as `ghost`. Carbon highlights via background colour token. - **Accessibility** ‚Äî Both meet WCAG. shadcn's `` includes a visible icon plus `More pages`; `` / `` carry `aria-label`s. ## Migration checklist 1. **Decide whether shadcn's primitive is enough.** If you need the Carbon-style "1‚Äì10 of 103 / items per page selector / page input" footer, you'll need to build it yourself ‚Äî shadcn only ships the link list. Often a `Pagination` + a sibling `` and own the `pageSize` state. 8. If you used `itemRangeText` / `itemsPerPageText`: render the strings yourself; consider an i18n helper. 9. If `Pagination` was inside a Carbon `DataTable` toolbar: it almost certainly needs the full Carbon-style footer; budget time to compose it.
